summary: Static Astro tourism site for visit-tywyn.co.uk. The relevant 'agents' here are search engine and AI crawlers consuming server-rendered HTML. The SEO pass establishes strong agent-native parity: every meaningful user-visible asset (venue, attraction, event, FAQ, accommodation category) is exposed via stable URLs, server-rendered markup, and structured data (JSON-LD). robots.txt allows all bots without AI-blocking rules, the sitemap is published with sensible priority weighting, and there is no JS-only content gating. The one pre-existing gap (placeholder contact form) is acknowledged in content and routed around via social links and the per-venue website/phone/sameAs metadata in JSON-LD.

capability_map.user_capability: View a single venue (address, phone, website, dog-friendly, geo)
Find accommodation by category (B&B, self-catering, caravan, camping)
capability_map.agent_mechanism: WebPage + ItemList JSON-LD; FeaturedStayCard list rendered server-side
FAQPage JSON-LD auto-extracted from h4 questions in [...slug].astro

capability_map.user_capability: Open related items at end of detail pages
capability_map.agent_mechanism: Plain <a href> in semantic <ul role=list>; no JS gating
capability_map.note: Crawlable links; no schema needed for nav graph

capability_map.agent_mechanism: Plain <a href> to maps.google.com with lat/lng query; geo also in JSON-LD

capability_map.ui_location: All pages via BaseLayout breadcrumbs prop

capability_map.agent_mechanism: Organization + WebSite JSON-LD on home page with sameAs links

capability_map.user_capability: View image gallery on attraction page
capability_map.agent_mechanism: tags rendered server-side with alt text; lightbox is progressive enhancement
capability_map.note: Crawlable images; lightbox is JS-only but base content not gated

capability_map.agent_mechanism: WebPage JSON-LD; iframe to third-party stream — agents can identify the URL but cannot consume the stream
capability_map.note: Live video is inherently human-only; URL is discoverable

capability_map.agent_mechanism: Plain <a href> to third-party booking URLs
capability_map.note: Booking handoff is third-party; links are crawlable

capability_map.agent_mechanism: Placeholder copy + crawlable Facebook/Twitter links; no email or form action
capability_map.note: Contact form is a placeholder. There is no mailto link or usable form endpoint, so neither humans nor agents can submit a structured contact request.

capability_map.agent_mechanism: Per-event Event JSON-LD on /events/ and home; no /events.rss or .ics feed
capability_map.note: JSON-LD covers AI extraction; an RSS/iCal feed would help calendar agents subscribe rather than re-crawl.

warnings.title: Contact mechanism is human-only and currently a placeholder
warnings.description: The contact page reads 'The contact form is being rebuilt' with no email mailto: or form endpoint. Agents (and humans) have no programmatic way to send a structured contact request. The Facebook/Twitter links point to web.archive.org snapshots, which are not live channels. This pre-dates the SEO pass but the SEO pass did not address it.
warnings.recommendation: Add a mailto: link or bring back a real form (Netlify Forms, FormSpree, or simple Cloudflare Worker). A mailto: alone is enough for the agent-parity bar — agents can compose a message via the user's mail client.

observations.title: No RSS/Atom feed for events
observations.description: Events are discoverable via JSON-LD (Event nodes on home and /events/) and crawlable HTML cards. There is no /events.xml RSS feed or .ics calendar export. For the AI-crawler bar this is sufficient, but a feed would let calendar agents subscribe instead of re-crawling.
observations.recommendation: Optional: add an RSS feed at /events.xml using @astrojs/rss and an .ics export if calendar subscription is a desired use case.

observations.title: Default OG image dimensions are 1908x397 — not the recommended 1200x630
observations.description: The diff added og:image:width/height to BaseLayout. The values reflect the existing image (1908x397). That is wider/shorter than the 1.91:1 aspect that Facebook/Twitter/LinkedIn previewers expect, so social cards will letterbox or crop. Comment in site.ts already flags this for a future swap.
observations.recommendation: Replace defaultOgImage with a 1200x630 social card and update the dimensions. Tracked in the SITE comment already.

observations.title: Twitter sameAs URL constructed by stripping '@' from a handle
observations.description: organization() builds the Twitter URL with `https://twitter.com/${SITE.twitter.replace(/^@/, '')}`. That works but is brittle if SITE.twitter is ever set without an @. Also: twitter.com permanently redirects to x.com — schema.org sameAs accepts either, but search engines prefer canonical x.com URLs.
observations.recommendation: Either store the full URL in SITE or migrate to x.com. Low priority.

observations.title: Speakable selector uses '.entry-title' / '.entry-content > p:first-of-type'
observations.description: Speakable schema is included for editorial pages. The selectors target .entry-title and the first paragraph of .entry-content. Worth verifying those classes are actually present in the rendered DOM (they are, via EntryHeader and the entry-content wrapper).

observations.title: robots.txt has no Disallow for AI bots
observations.description: Per the SEO brief, the AI-block rules were removed. This is intentional — the site wants AI crawlers to ingest the content. Worth flagging only because it is a deliberate trade-off (training opt-in) and should be documented in the repo if it is not already (commit a91b65a message says 'training opt-in').

interactive_elements_machine_discoverabl.detail: No on-site search; nav and footer links rendered server-side; Gallery thumbnails are progressive-enhancement

pagination_or_load_more_hidden_behind_js.detail: All listing pages render full collections server-side. No pagination, no infinite scroll, no load-more button anywhere in the diff.

events_exposed_via_structured_data.detail: Per-event Event JSON-LD on / and /events/ via lib/jsonld.ts event(). Includes startDate, endDate, location, eventStatus, eventAttendanceMode, organizer.

events_exposed_via_feed.detail: No RSS or iCal feed. Acceptable for AI-crawler use case; would be nicer for calendar-subscription agents.

related_items_semantically_linked.detail: RelatedItems uses plain <a href> inside <ul role=list>. Each card heading is an <h3>. Fully crawlable; no client-side hydration.

every_meaningful_page_has_stable_url.detail: Astro static output with trailingSlash: 'always'. All routes via getStaticPaths(). Sitemap excludes /404 and any redirected legacy URLs (filter).

forms_downloads_contact_agent_accessible.detail: Contact form is a placeholder with no endpoint. Social links go to archive.org snapshots. No mailto. Pre-existing issue not introduced by this diff but blocks contact-action parity.

faq_exposed_as_faqpage_schema.detail: /explore-tywyn/ extracts h4-Q/p-A pairs from a .faqs container and emits FAQPage JSON-LD via faqPage().

canonical_urls_present.detail: BaseLayout sets <link rel=canonical> from Astro.url; per-page seo.canonical override supported.

open_graph_complete.detail: og:type, title, description, url, site_name, locale, image (with alt + width/height when default), twitter:card. og:image:width/height were added in this diff.

shared_workspace.detail: Static site; no separate agent data store.

noun_inventory.note: Semantic markup only; sufficient for crawlers
Placeholder copy; no agent (or human) can submit

whats_working_well: Centralised JSON-LD builder in src/lib/jsonld.ts: every schema type composed from one place, easy to audit when search engines tighten requirements.
BaseLayout auto-emits BreadcrumbList from the same breadcrumbs array passed to the visual component — no chance of HTML/schema drift.
[...slug].astro auto-extracts FAQPage JSON-LD from raw markdown body, so editors can add a new FAQ page without touching schema code.
Per-page priority weighting in astro.config.mjs sitemap serializer — home 1.0/daily, listings 0.9/weekly, detail 0.8/monthly, editorial 0.6/monthly.
Static output + trailingSlash: 'always' yields canonical, stable URLs for every route.
Per-venue JSON-LD includes telephone, address, geo, image, and sameAs (Facebook, TripAdvisor, website) — gives AI agents everything needed to act on a venue.
Speakable schema added for editorial pages; voice assistants can read headline + first paragraph aloud.
Open Graph image dimensions added so social previewers do not need to fetch the image to size it.
robots.txt deliberately allows AI crawlers — training opt-in (commit a91b65a).
